articles.headline: Sensex closes above 75,000 for first time on global cues
articles.summary: The BSE Sensex breached the historic 75,000 mark on a closing basis for the first time, driven by strong buying in banking, metal, and energy stocks. The index rose 328 points to settle at 75,018, while the NSE Nifty 50 reached a near-record high of 22,829. Analysts attribute the rally to positive global sentiments and domestic market confidence ahead of election results.

articles.headline: Adani Group plans $90 billion capital expenditure over next decade
articles.summary: The Adani Group announced a massive capital expenditure plan of $90 billion over the next 10 years to strengthen its presence in airports, energy, and infrastructure. Group Chairman Gautam Adani stated that the investment would be largely funded through internal accruals. The conglomerate aims to play a key role in India's green energy transition and logistics sector expansion.

articles.headline: IMD issues red alert for severe heatwave in Northern India
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a red alert for several northern states as temperatures crossed 47 degrees Celsius in some regions. Severe heatwave conditions are expected to continue in Delhi, Rajasthan, Punjab, and Haryana for the next few days. Authorities have advised citizens to avoid outdoor exposure during peak hours and stay hydrated to prevent heat-related illnesses.

articles.headline: Rural FMCG consumption growth outpaces urban markets in Q4
articles.summary: Data from NielsenIQ shows that rural consumption of fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) grew at 7.6% in the March quarter, surpassing urban growth for the first time in five quarters. The recovery is attributed to moderating inflation and improved agricultural outcomes. Overall volume growth for the sector stood at 6.6%, led by strong demand in the home and personal care categories.

articles.headline: India’s steel exports to Europe face risk from new carbon tax
articles.summary: Indian steel manufacturers expressed concerns over the European Union's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), which could impose a 20-35% tax on high-carbon goods. Trade experts warn that these regulations could make Indian exports less competitive. The Indian government is currently in talks with EU officials to seek a waiver or mutual recognition of its own green initiatives.

articles.headline: Indian students remain top recipients of US study visas in 2023
articles.summary: According to the latest US Open Doors report, India remains the leading source of international graduate students in the United States. In the 2022-23 academic year, the number of Indian students increased by 35% to reach an all-time high of 268,923. The report highlights that one in every four international students in the US is now Indian, focusing primarily on STEM and management courses.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes easier rules for companies to comply with regulations
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) released a consultation paper aimed at easing compliance requirements for listed companies. Key proposals include relaxing the definition of 'connected persons' and streamlining reporting for market rumors. The move is part of the regulator's effort to enhance the ease of doing business while maintaining strict market integrity.

articles.headline: IMF raises China's 2024 growth forecast to 5% after strong Q1
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) upgraded China's economic growth forecast for 2024 to 5%, up from its previous estimate of 4.6%. The revision follows a stronger-than-expected first quarter and recent policy measures taken by the government to support the struggling property sector. However, the IMF warned of medium-term risks like an aging population and slowing productivity growth.

articles.headline: KKR enters IPL 2024 final with dominant win over SRH
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) secured their spot in the IPL 2024 final after an eight-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) in Qualifier 1. Mitchell Starc’s early breakthroughs restricted SRH to 159, which KKR chased down in just 13.4 overs. Shreyas Iyer and Venkatesh Iyer both scored unbeaten fifties to ensure a comfortable win at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

articles.headline: Delhi EV policy benefits long-term car owners, says CM Gupta
articles.summary: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta described the Delhi Electric Vehicle Policy 2026 as a "common man-centric" initiative, benefiting families owning a single vehicle for long periods. She urged residents to embrace electric mobility to improve the city's air quality, highlighting the government's plan to expand charging infrastructure.

articles.headline: US Justice Dept defends dropping Adani criminal case
articles.summary: The US Department of Justice defended its decision to abandon the criminal case against Gautam Adani and seven others, telling a federal judge the prosecution was legally flawed and diplomatically counterproductive. It argued the case "should have been dropped a year ago — or never brought in the first place."

articles.headline: ECI refuses to register Kavitha's Telangana Rakshana Sena
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India (ECI) declined to register former MLC K Kavitha's party, Telangana Rakshana Sena (TRS), due to numerous objections over its name, which closely resembles Telangana Rashtra Samithi (TRS). Kavitha argued the registration should not be denied based on objections.

articles.headline: India will continue building oil refineries: PM Modi
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi stated India will continue building new oil refineries to secure supply chains, crediting the sector's expansion for avoiding fuel shortages during the West Asia conflict. He highlighted India's rise as the fourth-largest refining nation, while other nations saw capacity declines.

articles.headline: New TMC West Bengal chief resigns in fresh blow for Mamata
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress's West Bengal state president Chandrima Bhattacharya resigned from all party positions, deepening the organizational crisis for Mamata Banerjee. This follows the party losing control of its headquarters and many senior leaders after the state elections defeat, with 58 of 80 legislators joining a rebel camp.

articles.headline: Vijayan asks SEBI to intervene in Adani's Vizhinjam port deal
articles.summary: Kerala Opposition Leader Pinarayi Vijayan wrote to SEBI and stock exchanges seeking intervention in Adani Group's proposed 49% stake transfer in Vizhinjam port, alleging breach of concession agreement, public interest, and national security issues. He claims Adani Group did not inform the state government.

articles.headline: Karnataka BJP alleges government obstructing electoral roll revision
articles.summary: Karnataka BJP lodged a complaint with the Election Commission, accusing the ruling Congress government of obstructing the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls by misusing Booth Level Officers (BLOs). BJP state president B Y Vijayendra alleged BLOs were pressured, not allowed to conduct door-to-door revisions.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh seeks private partners for Rushikonda resort
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh government invited EOIs from hospitality operators for the controversial ₹452-crore Rushikonda complex in Visakhapatnam, aiming to convert it into an international-standard hospitality destination via a PPP model. The property, once eyed as a CM's residence, remained vacant for two years.

articles.headline: TNCC chief criticizes DMK MLA over defamatory speech
articles.summary: TNCC chief Manickam Tagore criticized DMK MLA Anita R Radhakrishnan for his defamatory speech targeting Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay, saying the former minister lacked responsible political discourse. Radhakrishnan was arrested after his bail plea was rejected.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu begins ammonia gas extraction from seafood factory
articles.summary: Authorities in Tiruvallur initiated a multi-agency operation to safely extract ammonia gas from a seafood export company, where a leak last month killed 18 women migrant workers. Traffic was diverted, and workers evacuated, as safety teams execute the process.

articles.headline: Revanth Reddy backs Rahul Gandhi for PM, attacks KCR
articles.summary: Telangana CM A Revanth Reddy strongly pitched for Rahul Gandhi as Prime Minister in 2029, calling it a "historical necessity." He also targeted former CM K Chandrasekhar Rao, questioning the alleged wealth amassed by KCR's family.

articles.headline: DVAC seizes ₹31 lakh unaccounted cash in Tamil Nadu civic raids
articles.summary: Directorate of Vigilance and Anti-Corruption (DVAC) officials confiscated ₹31.27 lakh in unaccounted cash and detected ₹26.7 lakh UPI transactions during simultaneous searches at 41 municipal corporations, municipalities, and town panchayats across Tamil Nadu.

articles.headline: Three arrested for Nandi idol theft in Karnataka
articles.summary: Three men, including a youth from Kerala, were arrested in Bargur, Karnataka, for stealing two bronze Nandi idols from a temple. Forest Department personnel intercepted their vehicle and found the idols concealed. Experts will examine if they are antique pieces.

articles.headline: Three cops suspended for consuming liquor on duty in Kochi
articles.summary: Three Civil Police Officers were suspended in Kochi for allegedly consuming liquor while on duty at the Armed Reserve Camp in Tripunithura. The incident came to light during a senior officer's inspection.

articles.headline: Delhi CM outlines EV policy, infrastructure, and urban challenges
articles.summary: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta detailed the EV Policy 2026, aiming to improve air quality by boosting EV adoption. She discussed the ₹15,000 crore investment, plans for charging infrastructure, and efforts to regularize 1,531 unauthorized colonies and redevelop 700 jhuggi clusters, ensuring safety provisions.

articles.headline: Man bludgeons wife to death, then dies by suicide in Delhi
articles.summary: A 23-year-old woman was allegedly bludgeoned to death by her husband, who then died by suicide after consuming chemical tablets in west Delhi's Ranhola area. Police said the man assaulted his wife over phone suspicions, then left their three-year-old daughter with his mother before taking poison.

articles.headline: Delhi govt asks I-T Dept to join new secretariat development
articles.summary: The Delhi government has requested the Income Tax Department to collaborate on developing the new secretariat buildings at ITO. The plan involves combining PWD headquarters, Vikas Bhawan, and I-T land into a contiguous 17.5-acre site for an integrated administrative complex, including office space for I-T.

articles.headline: Delhi government ends two-day WFH system for staff
articles.summary: The Delhi government has withdrawn its two-day work-from-home (WFH) arrangement for most departments, citing that the "geopolitical situation has nearly normalised." Office timings revert to 10 am-6.30 pm from the previous 10.30 am-7 pm.

articles.headline: LG flags housing, traffic, water security as Delhi's urban priorities
articles.summary: LG Taranjit Singh Sandhu chaired a DDA advisory council meeting, highlighting housing shortages, unauthorized colonies, traffic congestion, water security, and climate resilience as key urban challenges for Delhi. Council members also raised issues like urban flooding and heritage structure neglect.

articles.headline: Gymkhana club member, staff challenge eviction notice in HC
articles.summary: A Gymkhana club member and its Staff Welfare Association have moved the Delhi High Court, challenging a show-cause notice issued by an estate officer regarding eviction proceedings. They contend the notice is based on "erroneous and premature assumptions" while their suit challenging a Centre's notice is pending.

articles.headline: Gurugram holds candlelight march for Delhi hit-and-run victim
articles.summary: Hundreds gathered in Gurugram for a candlelight march demanding justice for 34-year-old Sarthak Mattoo, killed in an alleged hit-and-run on June 25 in south Delhi. Mattoo's family, friends, and supporters called for a fair and expeditious investigation.

articles.headline: Building collapses in Chandigarh, three rescued, two trapped
articles.summary: Three workers were rescued after a two-storey building under renovation collapsed in Chandigarh’s Industrial Area Phase-II. Police said two others were still trapped, and rescue teams including NDRF personnel were on site.

articles.headline: E20 petrol safe for all vehicles, industry executives say
articles.summary: Industry executives sought to allay concerns over 20% ethanol-blended petrol (E20), asserting it's safe for new and older vehicles and crucial for reducing crude oil dependence. They confirmed rigorous scientific testing and no issues for E10 car components, despite a marginal performance impact.

articles.headline: PM Modi launches revamped UDAN civil aviation scheme
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched the next phase of the UDAN civil aviation scheme and inaugurated Jodhpur airport's new terminal. The modified scheme, with ₹29,000 crore outlay over 10 years, aims to expand regional aviation infrastructure and develop 100 aerodromes for last-mile connectivity.

articles.headline: Bihar upgrades Lalu Prasad, Rabri Devi security to Z category
articles.summary: The Bihar government upgraded the security cover of RJD chief Lalu Prasad and former chief minister Rabri Devi to Z category, hours after they moved to their private house. The decision includes a bulletproof car, following recommendations from the State Security Committee.

articles.headline: Amit Shah reviews Manipur security situation in Delhi
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah reviewed Manipur’s security situation in Delhi with top officials, including the state’s police chief and security advisor. The meeting directed all chiefs to work on bringing communities together, ensuring open highways, and cracking down on armed elements causing threats or extortion.

articles.headline: Discrimination persists in India's elite educational institutions
articles.summary: Despite equity regulations, discrimination remains prevalent in India's elite educational institutions, disproportionately affecting marginalized students, leading to suicides. A recent report revealed feeble gains since 2012 guidelines, with students from privileged groups dismissing bias while marginalized groups report evolving forms of prejudice and apathy.

articles.headline: Idiomatic differences in British and Indian English
articles.summary: The article explores how Indian English speakers often describe literally, while British English speakers use idioms, making their language richer and more understated. The author suggests Indians break into native languages for idiomatic expression, indicating English remains a foreign language they're not fully comfortable playing with.

articles.headline: Maharashtra set to recognise women farmers, addressing historical injustice
articles.summary: Maharashtra is poised to become the first Indian state to formally recognize women farmers, a move long-demanded to address their invisibilized labor and lack of rights. This bill could grant them access to government schemes, credit, insurance, and social security, enhancing their dignity and participation.

articles.headline: Travel humbles and offers perspective on life's problems
articles.summary: The author describes how travel to places like Bellagio, Italy, makes one feel insignificant, putting exaggerated personal problems into perspective. Monuments and natural wonders remind us of the universe's indifference to individual stress, which the author finds liberating, making life feel much bigger.

articles.headline: J&K suspends 8 officials over books glorifying separatists
articles.summary: J&K Lt Governor Manoj Sinha suspended eight School Education Department officials and removed one contractual staffer amid a row over two textbooks allegedly glorifying separatist leaders and convicted terrorists. The books, "Personalities and Legends of J&K" and "Great Personalities of Jammu and Kashmir," have been withdrawn.

articles.headline: Srinagar Airport withdraws full runway closure plan for repairs
articles.summary: Srinagar International Airport withdrew its proposed Notice to Airmen (NOTAM) for a full runway closure for repairs, ensuring uninterrupted daytime flight operations this year. Night closures for maintenance will continue until October 2026, with airport operations on all days from 0800 hrs to 1700 hrs.

articles.headline: Trump says WH meeting with Netanyahu likely next week
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump told Axios on Saturday that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had requested a meeting at the White House, likely to occur next week after the NATO summit in Turkey.

articles.headline: China promotes two generals, one to anti-graft chief
articles.summary: China's President Xi Jinping promoted two military officers, Zhang Shuguang and Wang Gang, to the rank of general. One of them was also appointed as the new head of the military’s top disciplinary and anti-graft body.

articles.headline: Egypt uncovers lost Byzantine-era city in western desert
articles.summary: Egypt announced the discovery of a preserved Byzantine-era city in the Dakhla Oasis, one of two major finds also including Marina el-Alamein. The unearthed city features a fourth-century basilica church and watchtowers.

articles.headline: Haroon Siddiqui reflects on five decades of Indian-Canadian journalism
articles.summary: Veteran Indian-Canadian journalist Haroon Siddiqui, 84, discusses his five-decade career, navigating identity, truth, and change. His memoir, "Unapologetic," traces his journey from India to Canada, covering wars and elections, emphasizing journalism's role in fostering independent thinking and promoting civil dialogue.

articles.headline: US at 250: The American Dream's evolution and challenges
articles.summary: As the US turns 250, the article examines the American Dream's four avatars – from land to entrepreneurship – and its constant promise of upward mobility. It questions the Dream's reality, highlighting persistent discrimination, wealth disparity, rising debt, and a precarious future amid global changes, urging for course-correction.

articles.headline: YouTube creators dominate horror film box office in 2026
articles.summary: YouTube creators are revolutionizing Hollywood, with horror films directed, co-written, and produced by them becoming 2026's highest-grossing. Titles like "Backrooms," "Obsession," and "Iron Lung," leveraging online fandom, bypass traditional studios, showcasing a cultural shift from spectatorship to participation in narrative building.

articles.headline: Black History Month marks 100 years of fighting selective amnesia
articles.summary: Marking its 100th year, Black History Month continues the fight against selective amnesia regarding Black experiences in the US. Despite progress like Black presidents and cultural influence, deep disparities persist in wealth, health, and life expectancy, highlighting the ongoing struggle to fulfill America's foundational premise of equality.

articles.headline: Food delivery waste harms health, environment; industry must innovate
articles.summary: The modern food delivery system generates significant food and packaging waste, contributing to methane emissions and chemical leaching into food. Minimum-spend thresholds and cheap water/carbon distort prices. The article advocates for industry innovation, electrified fleets, and sustainable options to combat the environmental and health costs.

articles.headline: Scientists study "aha moments" to understand brain insights
articles.summary: Researchers are using brain-imaging to study "aha moments" – abrupt changes in understanding – like Archimedes' Eureka. Discoveries suggest these insights involve shifts in brain activity, including visual recognition, pleasure (amygdala), and memory (hippocampus). Understanding this could boost learning, creativity, and AI development.

articles.headline: Decency and fairness reveal character in everyday interactions
articles.summary: The article reflects on how small interactions, like with service staff, reveal true character. It argues that many Indians default to power hierarchies rather than dignity of labor, treating service as subservience. The author emphasizes that acknowledging humanity and expressing gratitude are essential, regardless of status.

articles.headline: Asian Games-bound athletes to compete in Delhi series
articles.summary: All Indian track and field athletes bound for the Asian Games will participate in the final leg of the Indian Athletics Series, scheduled for September 10 in Delhi, as announced by the Athletics Federation of India.

articles.headline: Praggnanandhaa joint leader at Croatian Grand Chess Tour
articles.summary: Indian Grandmaster R Praggnanandhaa won three consecutive matches on the final day of the Croatian leg of the Grand Chess Tour's rapid section, finishing as joint leader with Firouzja Alireza of France, both on 12 points.

articles.headline: Sai Sudharsan's 168 leads India A to 541/8
articles.summary: Sai Sudharsan's fluent 168 and strong contributions from middle and lower-order batters helped India A reach a commanding 541/8 after a rain-affected Day 3 of the second unofficial Test against Sri Lanka A in Galle. India A now leads by 175 runs.

articles.headline: Morocco first to reach World Cup quarter-finals
articles.summary: Azzedine Ounahi scored a brace as Morocco defeated Canada 3-0 in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 match in Houston. Morocco will now face the winner of the Paraguay vs France game in the quarter-finals.

articles.headline: Asia's World Cup run ends, questions on performance raised
articles.summary: Asia's 2026 World Cup campaign concluded with Australia's defeat to Egypt in the Round of 32, highlighting a poor overall showing for Asian teams. Only Australia and Japan reached the knockouts, winning just three of 27 group stage matches, prompting calls for reassessment of development strategies.

articles.headline: Jurgen Klopp "ready" for Germany coach discussions
articles.summary: Jurgen Klopp stated he is "recharged" and "ready" for "intensive discussions" regarding becoming Germany's new coach, following Julian Nagelsmann's resignation after the World Cup exit. Klopp, under contract with Red Bull, reportedly has a verbal agreement to pursue the Germany job.

articles.headline: Zlatan Ibrahimovic supports Australian teen after World Cup penalty miss
articles.summary: Zlatan Ibrahimovic offered words of encouragement to Australian teenager Lucas Herrington, 18, after his penalty miss against Egypt in the World Cup Round of 32 shootout. Ibrahimovic called penalties a "lottery" and hailed Herrington's courage, saying, "You're 18-years-old, and you're young, this is just the beginning of your career."

articles.headline: Mexico City tightens security for World Cup match after deaths
articles.summary: Mexico City will double security and limit capacity at monuments and fan festivals for Sunday’s Mexico-England World Cup match, following four deaths during celebrations after Mexico's win over Ecuador. Mayor Clara Brugada announced measures to prevent similar tragedies.

articles.headline: Colombia beats Ghana 1-0, advances to World Cup last 16
articles.summary: Jhon Arias scored the only goal as Colombia defeated Ghana 1-0 in Kansas City, securing their place in the World Cup last 16. Colombia's disciplined defense extended their unbeaten run, setting up a clash with Switzerland in the next round.

articles.headline: US Startup Orbital Plans 100,000 Satellites for Space Data Centres
articles.summary: US-based startup Orbital has filed with the FCC to launch 100,000 satellites into low earth orbit to host data centres. The project aims to provide massive computing power for AI workloads in space, where cooling is efficient and solar energy is abundant. The company plans its first data centre satellite launch next year on a shared payload with SpaceX.

articles.headline: India-Peru FTA Talks Likely to Take Time Over Market Access
articles.summary: Negotiations for a proposed free trade agreement between India and Peru are unlikely to conclude soon due to differences over market access for certain products. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal stated that while talks are progressing, India will not compromise on quality control orders. Trade between the two nations has grown, but specific concerns on local industry protection remain.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank Gross Advances Grow 15.4% in June Quarter
articles.summary: HDFC Bank reported a 15.4% year-on-year growth in gross advances to ₹30.61 lakh crore for the quarter ended June 30, 2026. Deposits grew by 14.7% to ₹31.7 lakh crore during the same period. The bank's retail loans grew by 20% while commercial and rural banking loans increased by 17%. The performance indicates strong credit demand despite global macroeconomic headwinds.

articles.headline: E20 Ethanol Blending Concerns Drive Higher Premium Petrol Sales
articles.summary: Sales of high-octane premium petrol have increased by 5-10% as motorists avoid E20 blended regular fuel due to concerns over engine performance and maintenance. While E20 is becoming the default across India, premium fuel sales are rising despite costing up to ₹160 per litre. Oil companies report that premium fuel demand is outstripping retail network availability in some regions.

articles.headline: US DoJ Drops Bribery Charges Against Adani on Legal Grounds
articles.summary: The US Department of Justice has dropped criminal charges against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani and others, citing 'legal and pragmatic' reasons. The filing stated there was no point in pursuing the case as no investors suffered losses and there was no restitution to recover. The charges initially alleged a $250 million bribery scheme to obtain solar energy contracts in India.

articles.headline: PS5 Demand Surges as Gamers Upgrade for GTA VI Release
articles.summary: Demand for PlayStation 5 consoles has spiked across India as gamers prepare for the upcoming launch of Grand Theft Auto VI. Retailers report that PS5 stocks are selling out within days of arrival. GTA VI, scheduled for a late 2025 or early 2026 release, will launch only on current-generation consoles, forcing owners of older hardware to upgrade their systems now.

articles.headline: Home Ministry Designates 23 Pak-Based Operatives as Terrorists
articles.summary: The Union Home Ministry has designated 23 individuals linked to groups like Lashkar-e-Taiba and Jaish-e-Mohammad as terrorists under the UAPA. This update brings the total number of individuals designated under the act to 80. The move enables the NIA to freeze assets and impose travel restrictions on these operatives, who are accused of orchestrating various terror attacks in India.

articles.headline: ISIS Emerges as Major Threat to Taliban Stability in Afghanistan
articles.summary: The Taliban has identified ISIS-K as a major threat to Afghan stability and its own regime. Intelligence reports suggest ISIS is recruiting disillusioned fighters and conducting frequent attacks. The regional spillover of ISIS ideology has raised security concerns for neighboring countries, including India, regarding the potential for cross-border terror activities and radicalization.

articles.headline: PM Modi Blames UPA Roadblocks for Delayed Semiconductor Projects
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi stated that previous UPA government hurdles delayed India's semiconductor ambitions by over two decades. Speaking at the CG Semi OSAT facility in Gujarat, he highlighted that India is now building an entire electronics value chain. He noted that of the 12 semiconductor projects approved, three have already commenced commercial production under the current regime.

articles.headline: BJP Chief Nabin Urges UP Leaders to Intensify Grassroots Outreach
articles.summary: BJP National President Nitin Nabin has directed party leaders in Uttar Pradesh to intensify public outreach ahead of the 2027 assembly elections. Addressing a state-level meeting, he urged workers to counter 'negative politics' by highlighting the benefits of central and state welfare schemes. The party aims to ensure a strong presence at every booth to maintain its electoral dominance.

articles.headline: Congress Attacks RSS Over Ayodhya Ram Temple Donation Row
articles.summary: The Congress party has demanded an FIR against Ram Temple trust functionaries over allegations of embezzlement of donations. The party questioned the silence of the RSS on the issue, claiming it cannot distance itself from the controversy. Sanjay Raut of UBT Sena also criticized the trust, suggesting that the pride of the temple has been hurt by financial irregularities.

articles.headline: Manipur Pays Tribute to Deceased BJP MLA Vungzagin Valte
articles.summary: Manipur tribal leader and BJP MLA Vungzagin Valte was laid to rest in Churachandpur after passing away at 62. Valte had been undergoing medical treatment in Delhi after being brutally attacked in Imphal during the 2023 ethnic violence. Thousands of people gathered to pay their respects, marking the first time a Chief Minister visited the district since the unrest began.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee Dares Rebels to Join BJP Directly
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee has challenged internal rebels to leave the TMC and join the BJP instead of conspiring from within. She made the remarks while appointing Chandrima Bhattacharya as the new state president. Banerjee emphasized that she would not tolerate backstabbing and urged party workers to remain loyal as the party prepares for upcoming local challenges.

articles.headline: Chirag Paswan's LJP (RV) Targets Dalit Vote in UP Polls
articles.summary: Chirag Paswan's LJP (Ram Vilas) is planning to contest the upcoming Uttar Pradesh assembly elections, focusing on consolidating the Dalit and Pasi vote bank. The party, a member of the NDA, held a commemorative event in Lucknow to mark its entry into the state's political landscape. Paswan aims to leverage his party's 9.5% base in Bihar to expand its influence in neighboring UP.

articles.headline: Modi Visit Expected to Boost BJP Campaign in Punjab
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is scheduled to visit Punjab on July 13 to inaugurate the renovated Jalandhar Cantonment railway station and address a public gathering. The visit is seen as a major boost for the BJP's campaign in the state ahead of local polls. Party leaders expect the PM's presence to revitalize the cadre and address regional development concerns.

articles.headline: Bankipur and Datia Prepare for High-Profile Assembly Bypolls
articles.summary: The Election Commission has issued notifications for assembly bypolls in Bankipur (Bihar) and Datia (Madhya Pradesh) scheduled for July 15. The Bankipur seat fell vacant after BJP president Nitin Nabin was elected to the Rajya Sabha. In Datia, the contest follows the disqualification of a sitting MLA. Both seats are expected to see fierce contests between the NDA and opposition alliances.

articles.headline: India Tightens Scrutiny of Chinese Herbicide Imports
articles.summary: The Department of Revenue has instructed customs authorities to provisionally assess imports of glufosinate and its salts from China following complaints of anti-dumping. The herbicide, used for weed control in crops like soya and cotton, is allegedly being sold at prices that hurt domestic manufacturers. Importers must now furnish financial guarantees for any potential additional duties.

articles.headline: Geopolitics and Macroeconomic Factors Hit Office Leasing in Q2
articles.summary: New office leasing activity in India's top eight cities fell by 14.5% year-on-year in the second quarter of 2026. The decline is attributed to global geopolitical uncertainties and a cautious approach by occupiers. However, Global Capability Centres (GCCs) remained the primary growth engine, accounting for 38% of total leasing activity, with Bengaluru and Delhi NCR leading the market.

articles.headline: CBI Arrests Former CFO of Reliance Capital in Fraud Case
articles.summary: The CBI has arrested Amit Bapna, former CFO of Reliance Capital, in connection with an investigation into alleged bank fraud. Bapna is accused of diverting funds from Reliance Home Finance to other ADAG companies, causing losses to lending banks. He was previously arrested by the ED and is now being questioned regarding financial irregularities and violations of RBI guidelines.

articles.headline: Government Scraps TDS on Gift City Aircraft Lease Rentals
articles.summary: The CBDT has exempted airlines from deducting tax at source (TDS) on lease rentals paid to aircraft leasing companies based in Gujarat's GIFT City. This move aims to establish India as a global aircraft leasing hub and reduce the financial burden on domestic airlines. The exemption applies to both operating and financial leases, improving liquidity for carriers expanding their fleets.

articles.headline: Indian Travellers Shift Preference Toward East Asia Over West Asia
articles.summary: Indian tourists are increasingly choosing East Asian destinations like Thailand, Vietnam, and Japan over traditional West Asian favorites due to regional conflicts and easier visa access. Travel portals report a 30-40% surge in bookings to Southeast Asia. Thailand and Malaysia's visa-free entry policies have particularly boosted demand, while interest in European travel remains stable.

articles.headline: Death in a Vial: The Deadly Oxytocin Contamination Scandal
articles.summary: A deep-dive investigation reveals how spurious oxytocin vials, containing mostly water or contaminated substances, led to the deaths of five women in Kota. The probe into manufacturer Jackson Laboratories found severe GMP violations, fabricated test reports, and non-compliance with drug safety standards. Despite repeated warnings and past violations, the firm continued production.

articles.headline: Cape Verde: A Musical and Cultural Journey Through Sodade
articles.summary: An exploration of Cape Verde’s rich cultural heritage, focused on the melancholic 'sodade' music popularized by Cesária Évora. The article details the islands' history as a hub for the slave trade and its transformation into a vibrant landscape of volcanic peaks and bossa nova rhythms. It highlights Mindelo as the cultural heart, where the soul of the islands' unique identity is best felt.

articles.headline: The Evolution of the Nehru-Modi Jacket in Indian Fashion
articles.summary: The sleeveless bandhgala waistcoat, historically associated with Jawaharlal Nehru, has undergone a branding transformation into the 'Modi Jacket'. While the design remains consistent, its recent mass-market popularity is attributed to Narendra Modi’s consistent use and personalization of the garment. The piece analyzes how political branding can redefine traditional cultural attire.

articles.headline: Islamist Rebels Attack Strategic Towns in Northern Mali
articles.summary: Rebel groups in northern Mali have launched attacks on the towns of Gao and Sevaré, targeting government military installations. The Azawad Liberation Front claimed responsibility for the assault, which aims to capture a northern corridor. Mali's army has deployed reinforcements to the region as residents flee the escalating violence, marking a significant breach in recent security efforts.

articles.headline: America Celebrates 250th Independence Anniversary with Grand Displays
articles.summary: The United States marked its 250th anniversary of independence with nationwide celebrations, including a massive rally led by President Trump in Washington. The event featured military flyovers and historic reenactments. While millions celebrated with fireworks and parades, the milestone was also marked by political debate and record-breaking heatwaves across several states.

articles.headline: Pakistan Faces Financial Strain as Inflation Surges to 11.7%
articles.summary: Pakistan’s inflation rate hit 11.7% in May, leading to warnings of prolonged financial strain for the country. The State Bank of Pakistan reported that high energy costs and a weakened currency are driving the surge. The economic crisis is exacerbated by political instability and delayed foreign exchange inflows, raising concerns about the nation's ability to meet its debt obligations.

articles.headline: Fashion Playbook: Sporting Events Influence Global Wardrobe Trends
articles.summary: Major sporting events like the FIFA World Cup and Wimbledon are increasingly influencing mainstream fashion, a trend known as 'blokecore'. Luxury brands are partnering with sports organizations to create apparel that blends performance with high-end style. Athletes like Lewis Hamilton and Novak Djokovic are becoming cultural tastemakers, leading the way for sportswear as everyday luxury wear.

articles.headline: Corporate Veterans Exit C-Suites to Launch Own Startups
articles.summary: A growing trend in India sees senior corporate leaders from firms like Tech Mahindra and Tech Mahindra quitting their high-profile jobs to launch startups. These 'late-stage' founders are leveraging their decades of experience to build ventures in SaaS, AI, and consulting. Investors are showing increased interest in these veteran-led firms due to their industry knowledge and credibility.

articles.headline: Iga Swiatek Advances to Wimbledon Last 16
articles.summary: World Number 1 Iga Swiatek has reached the fourth round of Wimbledon after a straight-sets victory over Karolina Pliskova. Swiatek dominated the match with her powerful baseline play and efficient serving. She will next face Filipina sensation Alexandra Eala, who made history by becoming the first player from her country to reach the second week of a grass-court Grand Slam.

articles.headline: Djokovic Embraces 'Dad-Dancing' Fun on Way to 25th Major
articles.summary: Novak Djokovic progressed to the quarterfinals of Wimbledon with a victory over Stefanos Tsitsipas. The 39-year-old champion, seeking a record 25th Grand Slam title, entertained the crowd with his 'dad-dancing' celebration after the match. Djokovic stated that he is enjoying the game more than ever and remains focused on his goal of surpassing Federer's record of eight Wimbledon titles.

articles.headline: PM: India bucking trend by adding refining capacity
articles.summary: PM Modi stated India bucked global trends by boosting refining capacity by over 1,000 LMT in the last 10 years, reaching 285 MMTPA. This contrasts with other nations reducing capacity due to climate goals. The govt aims to raise capacity to 450 MMTPA by 2030, enhancing energy security and self-reliance, with L&T executing projects.

articles.headline: DWEES issues restrictions on use of drinking water
articles.summary: The Directorate of Water & Environmental Studies (DWEES) imposed restrictions on drinking water use due to falling groundwater levels. Prohibited uses include construction, car washing, road cleaning, and swimming pools. Violators face fines from ₹5,000 to ₹10,000, with repeat offenders facing disconnection of water supply.

articles.headline: EV buses collide, 13 passengers injured
articles.summary: Two electric buses collided at KR Circle at 8:30 am on Sunday, injuring 13 passengers. The incident occurred when an electric bus from Kempegowda Bus Station hit a stationary electric bus, resulting in shattered glass and loud screams. All injured are stable.

articles.headline: Pak, PoK operatives tagged as terrorists
articles.summary: The MHA declared 23 individuals, including Pakistan-based and PoK operatives, as "individual terrorists." This is part of India's strategy to designate terror masterminds and affiliates under the UAPA Act, targeting those involved in cross-border terrorism.

articles.headline: 30 killed in strike on Pak Canon Salepurup
articles.summary: 30 individuals were killed in an air strike in Pakistan's Canon Salepurup area near the Afghan border. The strike targeted a Lashkar-e-Islam hideout. Pakistan Army confirmed the casualties, stating the strike occurred Saturday night, but details are not yet clear.

articles.headline: Report in Justice Verma panel to be tabled this session: Birla
articles.summary: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla announced that the report of the Justice Verma committee, formed after the 2012 Delhi gang rape case, focusing on the protection of women and children, will be tabled in the upcoming Parliament session, emphasizing its significance for public debate and future legislation.

articles.headline: Despite warning, BLOs state wary to hit home visits or mass camps
articles.summary: Election Commission officials (BLOs) expressed reluctance to conduct door-to-door visits or mass camps in Bengaluru, citing safety concerns and lack of support, despite a recent directive. This affects the upcoming special summary revision of electoral rolls, essential for accurate voter lists.

articles.headline: Whistleblower held; panel says daycare centres must offer live streaming access
articles.summary: A whistleblower, who exposed alleged irregularities in a Bengaluru daycare, was detained. Concurrently, a committee proposed that all daycare centers in Karnataka provide live streaming access of their premises to parents, install CCTVs, and ensure 24x7 security, following recent child safety concerns.

articles.headline: Lifestyle of Ram Temple trust accused damaged financially
articles.summary: Champat Rai, general secretary of the Ram Temple Trust, claimed that his and his family's lifestyle was "damaged financially" due to allegations of corruption in land deals. He asserted that the trust, formed in 2020, acquired land legally, transparently, and at market rates, refuting charges of profiteering.

articles.headline: Canal work on E20: No engine damage, but mileage drops
articles.summary: A study by IISC and others found that canal work does not damage vehicle engines, but E20 fuel (20% ethanol blend) reduces mileage by 5-10%. The report, using 1.1 million kms of data, aims to address public concerns about the impact of E20 on vehicle performance and longevity.

articles.headline: Taps Under Watch As Rules Tightened
articles.summary: The Bangalore Water Supply and Sewerage Board (BWSSB) tightened rules on drinking water usage due to water scarcity. Prohibited activities include car washing, construction, and road cleaning, with fines from ₹5,000 to ₹10,000. Repeat offenders face water disconnection. Efforts include installing new borewells.

articles.headline: Contaminated Cauvery water sparks health fears
articles.summary: Bengaluru residents in areas like Rajaji Nagar and Basavanagudi reported contaminated Cauvery water, sparking health fears and increasing demand for bottled water. The BWSSB is investigating the issue, attributing contamination to pipeline bursts and sewage mixing, promising repairs and increased water quality checks.

articles.headline: Road Rage: Viral clips show Mercedes man assaulting motorist
articles.summary: A Bengaluru businessman, 43, was arrested for assaulting a motorist in a road rage incident, captured in viral dashcam footage. The victim alleged the Mercedes driver hurled abuses and hit him repeatedly. Police tracked down the accused using the vehicle registration.

articles.headline: Man arrested after woman, 25, alleges rape at b'day party
articles.summary: A Bengaluru man was arrested for allegedly raping a 25-year-old woman at his birthday party. The victim, who was inebriated, reported the incident after regaining consciousness. The accused, who works as a DJ, was apprehended based on her complaint.

articles.headline: Interim relief for platforms challenging gig workers' Act
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court granted an interim stay on certain provisions of the state's gig workers' welfare law, following petitions from various aggregator platforms. The law aims to provide social security and welfare benefits to gig workers, but platforms argue it burdens them excessively.

articles.headline: 2nd nanny gave contradictory statements on videos: Police
articles.summary: A second nanny provided contradictory statements regarding videos of a child being physically abused at a Bengaluru daycare. The police are investigating the conflicting accounts and seeking to register the daycare under the Karnataka Early Childhood Care and Education Policy, 2024.

articles.headline: Eggs-pensive times: Supply shortage pushes retail prices to seasonal high
articles.summary: Egg prices in Bengaluru surged to ₹7 per egg due to a 10-15% drop in supply, driven by increased demand during the wedding season, higher temperatures affecting bird health, and increased feed costs. Retailers anticipate prices to remain high for 15-20 days.

articles.headline: SHATTERED GLASS & LOUD SCREAMS: Electric bus rams stationary bus, leaves 18 injured
articles.summary: An electric bus collided with a stationary electric bus at KR Circle in Bengaluru, injuring 18 passengers. The driver of the moving bus claimed brake failure, while passengers recounted chaos. The accident prompted calls for improved bus maintenance and safety, with BMTC investigating.

articles.headline: HOME-BOUND JOURNEY ENDS IN HOSP: I THOUGHT I’D LOSE MY LEG: SURVIVOR
articles.summary: A 28-year-old survivor of the Bengaluru bus collision recounted the terrifying experience, fearing she would lose her leg after being pinned. Her husband struggled to rescue her, and she praised the quick response of local residents. She sustained a fracture and underwent surgery.

articles.headline: Padma Sharma gets K'taka Sahitya Academy's first translation lifetime award
articles.summary: Padma Sharma received the Karnataka Sahitya Academy's first Lifetime Award for Translation. Honored for her extensive contributions, including over 100 translated works from Kannada to Hindi and vice-versa, she aims to promote literary exchange and inspire new translators.

articles.headline: East B'luru's growth outpacing infra planning: Corpn chief
articles.summary: BBMP Chief Commissioner said East Bengaluru's rapid growth outpaced infrastructure development due to inadequate planning. The area, home to IT hubs and 40% of the city's population, faces challenges like traffic, waste, and water supply, with new projects underway to address these issues.

articles.headline: Woman jumps onto Metro tracks in R’nagar, rescued
articles.summary: A 24-year-old woman jumped onto the Metro tracks at Rajajinagar station in Bengaluru but was rescued by station staff and fellow passengers before a train arrived. She was unharmed and later handed over to her family. Metro services were briefly disrupted.

articles.headline: Bike-taxi accident victim, 32, bedridden
articles.summary: A 32-year-old woman, injured in a bike-taxi accident three months ago, remains bedridden with severe fractures. The driver, accused of speeding, is out on bail. The victim's family is struggling with medical expenses and seeking justice, highlighting concerns about bike-taxi safety.

articles.headline: Holes in probe: Why state’s drug battle loses its punch in court?
articles.summary: Data shows Karnataka's drug-related convictions fell from 34.2% in 2020-21 to 20.2% in 2024. Police cite gaps in investigation, inadequate evidence, and procedural lapses for the low conviction rates. This impacts the state's efforts to combat drug trafficking effectively.

articles.headline: SIR: Mobile linking rule for online forms sparks plaints
articles.summary: A new rule mandating Aadhaar-linked mobile numbers for online applications to the State Resident Information Registry (SIR) has sparked complaints. Citizens face issues like mismatched data and difficulty in updating numbers, hindering access to essential government services.

articles.headline: Official identity card rule on govt survey hits roadblocks
articles.summary: A rule mandating unique identity cards for government survey personnel in Karnataka faces implementation challenges. While aimed at ensuring transparency and accountability, concerns about data privacy and the logistical hurdles of issuing cards are delaying its rollout.

articles.headline: Assn: RTE reimbursement delays force fee-paying parents to shoulder burden
articles.summary: The Karnataka Registered Unaided Private Schools' Management Association (RUPSA) highlighted that delayed RTE reimbursement from the government forces schools to burden fee-paying parents. Over ₹500 crore is pending, affecting school operations and creating financial strain on both institutions and parents.

articles.headline: BLOs are seen carrying bags weighing up to 8kg
articles.summary: Booth Level Officers (BLOs) reported carrying heavy bags weighing up to 8kg during electoral survey work, causing physical strain. They requested better logistical support and smaller, lighter bags to ease their burden and improve efficiency in data collection.

articles.headline: Tourist rush: Illegal trek routes to Jog falls sealed
articles.summary: Authorities sealed illegal trek routes to Jog Falls in Shivamogga district due to safety concerns and environmental damage. The move aims to regulate tourism, prevent accidents, and preserve the natural ecosystem, with stricter enforcement planned to curb unauthorized access.

articles.headline: Months on, free insulin eludes diabetic children
articles.summary: Free insulin for diabetic children under the Karnataka government’s 'Manege Mantralaya' scheme remains largely inaccessible months after its announcement. Many families report being denied insulin due to eligibility issues or non-availability, leaving children vulnerable and families burdened by costs.

articles.headline: EV makers: Extend central govt scheme
articles.summary: EV manufacturers requested the central government extend the FAME II scheme, which incentivizes electric vehicle adoption, beyond March 2025. They argue the extension is crucial to sustain growth, boost local manufacturing, and achieve India's ambitious electrification targets amidst evolving market demands.

articles.headline: B'luru tops U30 startup list; Min
articles.summary: Bengaluru led India in the 'Under 30' startup ecosystem, with over 15,000 startups founded by individuals under 30. The IT/BT minister highlighted the city's robust innovation environment, government support, and talent pool as key drivers for this vibrant entrepreneurial success, attracting global attention.

articles.headline: Another blow to Didi, long-time confidante quits as TMC prez
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee faced another political setback as her long-time confidante and TMC's North Bengal President, Binay Krishna Tamang, resigned from the party. His departure ahead of crucial elections signifies internal party discontent and a potential challenge for TMC in the region.

articles.headline: India accounts for a fourth of forests lost globally
articles.summary: India accounted for 25% of global forest loss in the last decade, primarily due to agriculture, logging, and mining. The data highlights the urgent need for robust conservation efforts and sustainable land-use policies to mitigate deforestation and protect biodiversity.

articles.headline: Monsoon to continue in 'active phase' over central India: IMD
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) predicted the monsoon would remain in an "active phase" over central India for the next three days, bringing widespread rainfall. This forecast indicates continued heavy showers, potentially impacting agriculture and water levels in the region.

articles.headline: Toxic fumes: 13 hurt to rescue bull from well
articles.summary: Thirteen people were hospitalized after inhaling toxic fumes while attempting to rescue a bull that fell into a well in Chikkaballapur. The fumes, likely from decomposing organic matter, caused unconsciousness. The bull was eventually rescued by a specialized team.

articles.headline: Adani case should have never been brought: US DoJ
articles.summary: The US Department of Justice recommended against bringing a criminal case involving the Adani Group, citing insufficient evidence of intentional wrongdoing in allegations related to clean energy projects. This decision concludes an investigation that began with allegations of fraud.

articles.headline: Married thrice? Refused vax? You’re not eligible for govt dole, says Bengal CM
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee stated that women married thrice or those who refused Covid-19 vaccination would not be eligible for the state's Lakhsmi Bhandar financial scheme, causing controversy. Critics called the criteria discriminatory and arbitrary, sparking debate over government benefits.

articles.headline: Gangster Goldy Dhillon arrested in Sri in multi-agency op
articles.summary: Notorious gangster Goldy Dhillon was arrested in Sri Lanka during a multi-agency operation involving Interpol, Indian intelligence, and Sri Lankan police. Dhillon, linked to multiple crimes including murder and extortion, will be extradited to India, marking a significant win against organized crime.

articles.headline: Waseypur gangster's hideout razed: MP, MLA report three
articles.summary: Authorities razed the alleged hideout of a Waseypur gangster, following complaints from local MP and MLA. The action aims to dismantle criminal networks and restore law and order, sending a strong message against illicit activities in the region.

articles.headline: Temple trust meet tomorrow, bringing in a CEO on agenda
articles.summary: The Ayodhya Ram Temple trust is set to meet to discuss appointing a CEO and implementing administrative reforms. The meeting will address audit observations, governance issues, and the need for professional management to ensure transparency and efficient operation of the temple.

articles.headline: Deposits to temple trust’s bank accounts were ₹16L to ₹18L per day
articles.summary: The Ayodhya Ram Temple trust's bank accounts receive daily deposits ranging from ₹16 lakh to ₹18 lakh, averaging ₹45-50 crore monthly. The funds, primarily from devotees, highlight the temple's significant financial inflows and the public's continued support.

articles.headline: Probe ordered into 'donation theft' at Badrinath temple, report in 10 days
articles.summary: Uttarakhand CM ordered an inquiry into alleged donation theft at Badrinath temple, with a report due in 10 days. The probe was initiated after complaints of missing gold and silver offerings, aiming to investigate financial irregularities and ensure accountability within the temple administration.

articles.headline: Govt fancies chances over Constitution bill
articles.summary: The government expressed confidence in passing a Constitution Amendment Bill to streamline the appointment of judges, despite opposition concerns. The bill aims to reform the collegium system, asserting greater executive role, but critics worry about judicial independence.

articles.headline: PM: Govt expanded gas network
articles.summary: PM Modi highlighted the government's expansion of the national gas pipeline network by over 20,000 km to nearly 50,000 km, reducing reliance on imported gas and promoting clean energy. This infrastructure push supports industrial growth and household access to natural gas.

articles.headline: Anybody dreaming of Tamil nation must be mentally ill: Madras HC
articles.summary: The Madras High Court observed that anyone "dreaming of a Tamil nation" must be "mentally ill," while dismissing a plea seeking a separate nation for Tamils. The court stressed the importance of national integrity and criticized calls for secession.

articles.headline: Aditya-L1 spots X-ray glow from strongest solar flares
articles.summary: India's solar observatory, Aditya-L1, detected X-ray glow from the strongest solar flares (X-class and M-class) using its HEL1OS payload. This scientific observation offers crucial insights into solar dynamics, helping understand space weather and its impact on Earth, showcasing the mission's success.

articles.headline: Now, govt eyes flex-fuel vehicles as way forward
articles.summary: The government is promoting flex-fuel vehicles (FFVs) to reduce crude oil imports and carbon emissions. Nitin Gadkari emphasized the need for car manufacturers to accelerate FFV production, which can run on petrol and ethanol blends, supporting energy self-reliance and environmental goals.

articles.headline: Can't consider bail plea on any ground: Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court refused bail to Sharjeel Imam and Umar Khalid, citing the Supreme Court's order staying proceedings under UAPA Section 13(1). This decision aligns with the apex court's directive, preventing lower courts from granting bail in similar cases until the legal position is clarified.

articles.headline: 36 years later, what Sarla Bhasin’s photo asks of us
articles.summary: An old photograph of Sarla Bhasin, a feminist scholar, sparked reflection on her life and work after being shared on social media. The article explores her impact on women's rights and social justice movements, prompting discussions about her legacy and relevant societal issues.

articles.headline: Are we saying a piece of writing can’t be that polished unless AI wrote it?
articles.summary: The Man Booker International Prize winner, Jenny Erpenbeck, raised concerns about AI's role in literature. She noted an AI-generated summary of her work showed a lack of human imagination, prompting debate about AI's limitations in creative writing and its potential impact on author perception.

articles.headline: Diaspora is reshaping the World Cup, but it can’t lift India’s football fortunes
articles.summary: The Indian diaspora's significant presence and influence in various national football teams are reshaping the World Cup, yet India itself struggles in the sport. The article explores how this global participation contrasts with India's domestic football challenges, despite its massive population.

articles.headline: Schrödinger’s citizens: When every document, but none is enough
articles.summary: The article highlights the complex and often frustrating process for individuals proving Indian citizenship, requiring numerous documents yet often facing rejections. It critiques the bureaucratic hurdles and inconsistencies, leaving many in a "Schrödinger's cat" state of uncertain belonging.

articles.headline: Going Dutch, and the problem it creates for Indian exports
articles.summary: The "going Dutch" trend, where costs are split, is impacting Indian exports to the Netherlands. The article explains how Dutch economic practices and trade policies, including stringent import regulations, create challenges for Indian businesses trying to access the European market.

articles.headline: Teen accused in two murders to be tried as adult: Juvenile Justice Board
articles.summary: The Juvenile Justice Board ruled that a 16-year-old accused in two murders in Bengaluru will be tried as an adult. The decision, based on the gravity of the crime and the minor's mental maturity, highlights a shift in how serious juvenile offenses are handled in India.

articles.headline: MHA tags 17 from Pak and 6 Indians ‘individual terrorists’
articles.summary: The MHA designated 17 Pakistan-based and 6 India-based individuals as "individual terrorists" under UAPA. This move targets those involved in terror financing, recruitment, and cross-border operations, aiming to strengthen India's counter-terrorism framework and disrupt militant networks.

articles.headline: 'Celebratory firing feeds gun culture': Court as it jails MLA
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court sentenced MLA Vinay Kulkarni to six months imprisonment for celebratory firing, stating such acts promote gun culture. The ruling emphasizes the need for responsible conduct by public figures and sends a strong message against misuse of firearms.

articles.headline: Justice Varma had claimed ignorance about a house in his name
articles.summary: Retired Justice ML Varma claimed ignorance about a property registered in his name in Bengaluru during the Lokayukta investigation. This arises amidst allegations of undisclosed assets, raising questions about his financial transparency and potentially impacting ongoing legal proceedings.

articles.headline: Insta Jalebiwallahs
articles.summary: The article explores the trend of "Jalebiwallahs" on Instagram, individuals who curate content around food, lifestyle, and culture. It discusses how these influencers connect with audiences, build communities, and impact consumer trends through visually appealing and relatable posts.

articles.headline: Can digital twins help athletes win? How AI is changing sport
articles.summary: Digital twins, powered by AI, are being used to create virtual replicas of athletes, analyzing biomechanics and performance data to optimize training and prevent injuries. This technology is revolutionizing sports science, offering personalized insights to enhance athletic success.

articles.headline: The volcanic wit behind Iceland Cricket's viral hits
articles.summary: Iceland Cricket gained global fame through its humorous and witty social media posts, making it a viral sensation despite being a minor cricketing nation. The article highlights their unique approach to engagement, combining self-deprecating humor with genuine love for the game.

articles.headline: Blue Sharks' fairytale debut campaign ends, but not before making its mark
articles.summary: The Blue Sharks, Cape Verde's football team, concluded their debut World Cup campaign, impressing with their performance and achieving their goal of putting the nation on the global football map. Their resilient play defied expectations, marking a significant milestone for African football.

articles.headline: Messi light up the game Philips
articles.summary: Lionel Messi's exceptional performance in the World Cup highlighted his enduring skill and leadership, drawing immense viewership. The article, sponsored by Philips, celebrates his ability to elevate the game, underscoring his impact on global football and brand partnerships.

articles.headline: New Round New Ground
articles.summary: The article discusses the evolution of World Cup football, noting rule changes and shifts in player roles over time, emphasizing how adapting to these changes is crucial for teams. It highlights the strategic adjustments teams make to stay competitive in modern football.

articles.headline: Egypt 'in the zone' with perfect confidence
articles.summary: Egypt's football team expresses high confidence for their upcoming match against Morocco, believing they are "in the zone" to deliver a strong performance. The team's focused mindset and preparation are key factors as they aim for victory in the crucial encounter.

articles.headline: Goals remain a concern for steady Colombia
articles.summary: Colombia's football team, despite solid defensive play, continues to struggle with goal scoring, raising concerns for their World Cup prospects. The team acknowledges the need to improve their offensive strategies to convert opportunities into goals and advance further in the tournament.

articles.headline: When England last visited Azteca...
articles.summary: The article reminisces about England's last match at Estadio Azteca, Mexico, in the 1986 World Cup, famously featuring Maradona's "Hand of God" goal. It contrasts past encounters with current team dynamics, reflecting on historical football moments and their lasting impact.

articles.headline: Recharged Klopp deepens German job
articles.summary: Jürgen Klopp, rejuvenated after a break, is reportedly deepening his involvement with the German national team, taking on a more significant role. His enhanced leadership and strategic insights are expected to boost the team's performance and morale ahead of major tournaments.

articles.headline: Brazil face Norway hoodoo in last 16
articles.summary: Brazil faces Norway in the World Cup Round of 16, a team they have historically struggled against, having never beaten them in major competitions. This "hoodoo" adds pressure to Brazil as they seek to break their winless streak against Norway.

articles.headline: Bethell spoils Vaibhav's big day
articles.summary: Tennis player Toby Bethell defeated India's Vaibhav Singh in a crucial match, spoiling Singh's opportunity for a significant win. The article details the match highlights and outcomes, emphasizing Bethell's performance and the disappointment for Singh.

articles.headline: Diganth powers City Cricketers
articles.summary: Diganth's powerful batting performance led City Cricketers to a significant victory against United Cricketers, scoring 100 runs off 59 balls. His century was crucial in chasing a challenging total, highlighting his impact on the team's success.

articles.headline: Zuccaro for main event
articles.summary: Wrestler Zuccaro is set to compete in the main event of a major wrestling championship, creating significant anticipation among fans. His participation is expected to draw a large audience, highlighting his prominent role in the sport.

articles.headline: Double for Daniel & Jhamna
articles.summary: Daniel and Jhamna clinched double victories in their respective swimming events, showcasing their dominance in the competition. Their impressive performances earned them multiple medals, highlighting their talent and rigorous training.

articles.headline: Prez Trump extols America, rails at communism in USA 250 celebrations
articles.summary: Former President Trump delivered a fiery speech at USA 250 celebrations, praising American exceptionalism while railing against communism. He emphasized themes of national pride and criticized progressive policies, aligning with his political rhetoric ahead of potential future campaigns.

articles.headline: Baloch group claims killing 30 Pak military personnel
articles.summary: A Baloch militant group claimed responsibility for killing 30 Pakistan military personnel in an attack in Balochistan. The incident highlights ongoing insurgency in the region, with the group demanding greater autonomy and control over local resources. Pakistan's military confirmed the attack.

articles.headline: Mass grief at funeral projects hardship grip on post-war Iran
articles.summary: Mass funerals for victims of a recent incident in Iran underscore the deep grief and economic hardship gripping the post-war nation. The events highlight societal unrest, challenging political narratives and revealing widespread public dissatisfaction amidst ongoing crises.

articles.headline: Russia says its taken five villages in Ukraine
articles.summary: Russia claimed to have captured five more villages in Ukraine's Donetsk region, intensifying its offensive. Ukraine disputed the extent of Russian advances. The fighting continues to be fierce, with both sides reporting heavy casualties and strategic gains in the ongoing conflict.

articles.headline: McDonald's cabin turn warehouse clinics in Venezuela
articles.summary: Former McDonald's cabins in Venezuela have been repurposed into warehouse clinics, providing basic healthcare services amid the country's economic and political crisis. This transformation reflects the severe shortage of medical facilities and the innovative ways communities are coping.

articles.headline: Firefighters battling a fungal forest blaze
articles.summary: Firefighters are battling a fungal forest blaze that has engulfed thousands of acres in a national park. The unique nature of the fungal fire, resistant to traditional extinguishing methods, poses a severe environmental threat and highlights challenges in combating novel ecological disasters.

articles.headline: Indian student drowns in Germany; four questioned
articles.summary: An Indian student drowned in a lake in Germany, and four friends have been questioned as part of the investigation. The incident has prompted calls for greater safety awareness for international students abroad and raised concerns among the Indian community.

articles.headline: JUST & MARRIED: Swift, Kelce host massive NY wedding bash
articles.summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce reportedly hosted a massive wedding bash in New York, attended by numerous celebrities. The event follows intense speculation about their relationship and potential marriage, marking a high-profile celebration for the celebrity couple.

articles.headline: Not easy for any child to live in two homes. But I never let Indira feel torn
articles.summary: Former model Madhu Sapre discussed co-parenting her daughter Indira after divorce, emphasizing efforts to ensure Indira never felt torn between two homes. Sapre highlighted the challenges and emotional complexities, sharing insights into maintaining a stable environment for children post-separation.

articles.headline: Lunchbox 'making' is morning's harder parents
articles.summary: The task of preparing children's lunchboxes is becoming more challenging for parents due to stricter school food policies, increasing food allergies, and demands for nutritious, appealing meals. This daily morning ritual often adds stress, requiring creativity and careful planning.

articles.headline: Will make more than care, says expert
articles.summary: An expert emphasized that modern daycare centers must offer more than just basic care, advocating for comprehensive early childhood development programs. They stressed the importance of quality education, trained staff, and stimulating environments to foster holistic growth in children.

articles.headline: Thousands Gather in Tehran for Ayatollah Khamenei’s Funeral
articles.summary: A massive state funeral was held in Tehran for Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who passed away at 85. Thousands of mourners gathered to pay their final respects as his body was taken to the holy city of Qom for burial. The transition of power comes at a critical time for Iran, with global leaders closely watching for indications of the regime's future political direction.

articles.headline: 15-Year-Old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Becomes India's Youngest Cricket Debutant
articles.summary: Bihar-born Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has made history as India's youngest international cricket debutant at age 15. Surpassing Sachin Tendulkar's long-standing record, Sooryavanshi featured in a T20I match against England. The young left-handed batsman's debut is seen as a significant moment for Indian cricket as it looks toward nurturing teenage talent for the future international stage.

articles.headline: Bihar BJP MLA sentenced to four years jail for celebratory firing death
articles.summary: A Delhi court sentenced Bihar BJP MLA Raju Kumar Singh to four years in prison for the death of Archana Gupta in celebratory firing at his farmhouse in 2018. The judge slammed the gun culture, stating the punishment serves as a deterrent against reckless firing.

articles.headline: Child's right to know parentage outweighs stigma, rules Delhi HC
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court ruled that a child's right to establish biological parentage takes precedence over concerns of social stigma or adults' reputational interests. Upholding a DNA test order, the court stated children cannot be casualties of adult choices, as parentage is fundamental to identity and legal entitlements.

articles.headline: UP police to seek custody of accused in Ram Temple donation theft
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh Police will seek custody of Lavkush Mishra and Anukalp Mishra, accused in the Ram Temple donation controversy, to investigate the theft mechanism and asset acquisition. This follows questioning of Avinash Shukla and a statement from the SBI branch manager in Ayodhya regarding alleged irregularities.

articles.headline: Cape Verde Stuns Argentina in Historic World Cup Match
articles.summary: In a major World Cup upset, Cape Verde defeated favorites Argentina 3-1. The match featured a standout performance by Cape Verde's goalkeeper Vozinha and a stunning long-range goal by Cabral. The victory marks the first time a team from the small island nation has defeated a former champion, throwing the group standings into chaos and delighting fans worldwide.

articles.headline: Modi Congratulates Trump on US 250th Independence Anniversary
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi extended his congratulations to US President Donald Trump and the American people on the historic 250th anniversary of US Independence. Modi highlighted the shared belief in democracy and the potential for the two nations to work together for global good. He expressed hope for a partnership that brings peace and prosperity for the next 250 years.

articles.headline: Swiatek dumped from Wimbledon by Eala, Serena withdraws
articles.summary: Iga Swiatek, Wimbledon's defending champion, was shocked in the third round by the Philippines' Alexandra Eala (29th seed), who won 7-6, 6-2. Serena Williams also withdrew from doubles due to a knee injury. Second seed Elena Rybakina crashed out, opening up the women's singles draw.

articles.headline: MHA designates 23 LeT, JeM operatives as terrorists
articles.summary: The Union Home Ministry designated 23 Pakistan-based Lashkar-e-Tayyeba and Jaish-e-Mohammed operatives as terrorists under UAPA. These individuals, 16 Pakistani and 7 Indian, are involved in anti-India activities, terror attacks, recruitment, arms trafficking, and fundraising. Mohammad Shahid Faisal, linked to blasts, is among them.

articles.headline: Manipur CM attends Kuki MLA's funeral amid boycott calls
articles.summary: Manipur CM Yumnam Khemchand Singh attended the last rites of BJP MLA Vungzagin Valte in Churachandpur despite Kuki organizations' boycott. This first CM visit since 2023 violence aimed to pay respects to Valte, who died after a mob attack. Kuki groups demand justice for his murder.

articles.headline: Court cites SC order, denies bail to Sharjeel, Umar
articles.summary: Delhi High Court denied bail to Sharjeel Imam and Umar Khalid in a 2020 Delhi riots case, citing a Supreme Court order that pauses proceedings against those charged under Section 13(1) of UAPA until the SC rules on the provision's constitutionality. The court referenced the 'Anant Prakash Pathak' case.

articles.headline: PM Modi inaugurates CG Semi OSAT facility in Sanand, Gujarat
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the CG Semi Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test (OSAT) facility in Sanand, Gujarat, emphasizing India's goal to build a complete electronics value chain. He stated the plant aims for 20 crore units annually, scaling to 500 crore, boosting Make in India.

articles.headline: Justice Varma Inquiry Report to be Tabled in Monsoon Session
articles.summary: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla announced that the inquiry committee report on allegations against former Delhi High Court Judge Justice Yashwant Varma will be tabled in the upcoming Monsoon Session. The probe followed the discovery of unaccounted cash at his official residence. The report will determine if the House initiates impeachment proceedings or other disciplinary actions.

articles.headline: Telegram Told to Act Against Piracy or Face Action
articles.summary: The I&B Ministry has issued a notice to Telegram directing the platform to remove pirated content including films and OTT material within 15 days. Government policy shifts aim to hold platforms accountable through proactive piracy detection and disabling infringing user accounts. Non-compliance could lead to legal action under the Information Technology Act and Copyright Act.

articles.headline: Second accused arrested in Bengaluru daycare abuse case
articles.summary: Bengaluru Police made a second arrest, identifying Sujatha, in the alleged physical abuse and torture of toddlers at a daycare center inside an IT company's campus in Brookefield. Sujatha was sent to judicial custody for 14 days following video evidence.

articles.headline: India-Israel Bilateral Investment Treaty Comes into Effect
articles.summary: A new bilateral investment treaty between India and Israel has come into effect, aimed at promoting and protecting investments between the two nations. The agreement includes a dispute resolution mechanism and protects the host state's right to regulate in public interest. Since 2000, India has received $311 million in FDI from Israel, primarily in the technology and agriculture sectors.

articles.headline: Oil Tankers Divert to Iran Route Amid Hormuz Tensions
articles.summary: Around 34 commercial vessels have reportedly diverted from the traditional Strait of Hormuz lanes to routes through Iranian waters. This shift comes as insurance costs for the Persian Gulf spike due to recent maritime incidents and geopolitical friction. While some tankers have returned to normal routes, the risk tolerance of global shippers remains tested by regional instability.

articles.headline: St Petersburg region oil terminal hit by major drone attack: Kyiv
articles.summary: Ukraine claimed responsibility for a major drone attack that hit an oil terminal in Russia's St. Petersburg region. The strike caused significant damage and sparked a fire, marking a notable escalation in drone warfare targeting Russian infrastructure.

articles.headline: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Marry in Private Ceremony
articles.summary: Pop star Taylor Swift and NFL player Travis Kelce were married in a private ceremony at Madison Square Garden. The event was attended by close friends and family, including actor Adam Sandler and singer Stevie Nicks. The surprise wedding followed months of media speculation about the couple’s relationship. No official photos have been released as the couple sought a quiet celebration.

articles.headline: Australia Pursue Record 7th Women's T20 World Cup Crown
articles.summary: Australia's women's cricket team has reached the final of the T20 World Cup after defeating South Africa in the semifinals. The defending champions will face England at Lord's in the title clash. Australia is seeking a record seventh crown, while England hopes to leverage home advantage to win their first T20 World Cup trophy since the inaugural tournament in 2009.

articles.headline: Antonelli Wins F1 Sprint, Hamilton Secures Second Place
articles.summary: Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli secured a victory in the British Grand Prix Sprint, edging out veteran teammate Lewis Hamilton. Hamilton finished second after a fierce battle on the final lap, while Lando Norris took third. The result helps Antonelli close the gap in the championship standings, setting up an intense final race day at the Silverstone circuit.
